## Visual proof
Video: 
[Screencast From 2026-05-20 11-38-07.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/da5eccc9-3061-4bec-8069-e2de24f698a0)

Shows the new opt-in Slack native progress experience:
  - A native Slack plan/task card appears while OpenClaw is still working.
  - The card updates as the tool-backed trip comparison progresses.
  - The task card completes before the final answer is posted.
  - The final answer lands in the same Slack thread after the native progress card.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Slack native progress task cards are opt-in through Slack-only `channels.slack.streaming.progress.nativeTaskCards=true`; existing Slack progress mode remains on the portable draft-preview path unless that flag is set. The opted-in native path sends Slack stream chunks (`plan_update` / `task_update`) instead of replacing full Block Kit messages.
Real environment tested: local source-run OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch at proof head `f8d834382cc7f429f73d03cf1095ea46831d216b`; cleaned head `1725061aba3b0a7c1f0d2b93cd0d1db5ed5ea040` had the same tree `96359c125f93975d3e9de2c3d35d73a03359e81a`. Current head `275035024c814cb6b70c00f99eade86ac7b49f52` includes the patch-progress identity rank-up, the post-rebase Slack CI/type fixes, the Codex review fix that keeps final-only native-progress turns on normal final delivery instead of Slack text streaming, and a scope cleanup that removes the unrelated OpenRouter lint change from the PR diff after rebasing onto latest `origin/main`. Slack workspace `4ok`, channel `C0ASNARACMT`, test sender message mentioning OpenClaw test app using OpenClaw test app credentials. For this proof, the source-dev Slack config was in `streaming.mode="progress"` with `streaming.progress.nativeTaskCards=true`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: started the local source-run gateway with OpenClaw test app credentials and a redacted HTTP-level Slack stream recorder, verified `/healthz` returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}` and logs showed `slack socket mode connected`; ran ingestion smoke marker `SMOKE-HEAD3-20260520T090802Z`; then ran `MARKER=SEQ38-HEAD-20260520T090828Z STREAM_ARTIFACT=/tmp/openclaw-SEQ38-HEAD-20260520T090741Z-stream-calls.jsonl ARTIFACT=/tmp/openclaw-SEQ38-HEAD-20260520T090828Z-slack-proof.json /tmp/openclaw-slack-proof.py`.
Evidence after fix: Slack thread `https://4okworkspace.slack.com/archives/C0ASNARACMT/p1779268109549229`; proof artifact `/tmp/openclaw-SEQ38-HEAD-20260520T090828Z-slack-proof.json`; redacted stream-call artifact `/tmp/openclaw-SEQ38-HEAD-20260520T090741Z-stream-calls.jsonl`; pass flags were `final_text_seen=true` and `stream_chunk_proof=true`. The stream artifact shows `chat.startStream` at `2026-05-20T09:08:43.966Z` with `task_display_mode=plan` and chunks `plan_update` + `task_update`, where the task status was `in_progress`, followed by multiple `chat.appendStream` requests carrying `plan_update` + `task_update` chunks before `chat.stopStream` at `2026-05-20T09:09:10.794Z`.
Observed result after fix: OpenClaw test app consumed the test sender prompt, Slack native progress was delivered through stream chunks while the command was still running, the native tasks later updated to `complete`, and the Slack thread received the exact final marker reply `FINAL-SEQ38-HEAD-20260520T090828Z`.
What was not tested: screenshot/video capture of the transient Slack UI; `conversations.replies` does not expose pre-stop stream chunk contents reliably, so proof uses redacted Slack stream request evidence plus final thread readback. Repeated same-name tool calls without upstream stable IDs may still collapse, and a failed command followed by a normal final assistant reply may finalize the native task as complete rather than error; both are intentionally left out of scope to keep this PR limited to the opt-in Slack-native progress feature.

target.title: Add CLI tool plan execution
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a deterministic tool-plan execution CLI that replays planned tool calls through the existing Gateway `tools.invoke` boundary instead of creating a parallel dispatcher.

- adds `executeToolPlan` with array and `{ steps }` plan support
- adds `openclaw tools execute-plan [file]` with JSON output, session/agent targeting, approval reporting, and stop-on-block defaults
- wires the new `tools` command into lazy CLI registration and command descriptors
- documents the new CLI page and navigation entry

## Why

This gives integrations and tests a stable way to take model-selected tool steps and dispatch them through OpenClaw's native tool invocation path, preserving normal tool resolution, approval, policy, and `before_tool_call` hook behavior before any tool body runs.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw tools execute-plan` can replay a JSON tool plan through the existing Gateway `tools.invoke` boundary, including plugin-owned tools and `before_tool_call` blocking before the tool body runs.

Real environment tested: Windows 11, Node v24.15.0, OpenClaw source branch `codex/execute-plan-cli` at commit `8e0ecd4275`, foreground Gateway from `openclaw gateway run` on `ws://127.0.0.1:18893`, token auth, one temporary native OpenClaw plugin loaded from `plugins.load.paths`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```powershell
node --import tsx src\entry.ts --profile pr-proof tools execute-plan .artifacts\execute-plan-plugin-proof-plan.json --url ws://127.0.0.1:18893 --token local-proof-token --json --timeout 120000
```

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the command above:
```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "stopped": true,
  "stopReason": "blocked_tool",
  "steps": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "action": "pr_allowed_tool",
      "status": "completed",
      "durationMs": 5990,
      "source": "plugin",
      "output": {
        "content": [
          {
            "type": "text",
            "text": "allowed tool body executed"
          }
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "index": 1,
      "action": "pr_blocked_tool",
      "status": "blocked",
      "durationMs": 4141,
      "error": {
        "code": "forbidden",
        "message": "PR proof blocked before tool body"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Plugin-side execution log from the same run:
```text
2026-05-15T20:33:12.191Z EXECUTE pr_allowed_tool input=before-block
2026-05-15T20:33:16.354Z BLOCK pr_blocked_tool input=must-not-run
```

Observed result after fix: The new CLI executed the first plugin tool through native Gateway `tools.invoke`, then the plugin `before_tool_call` hook blocked the second planned tool with `forbidden`. The blocked tool body did not log an `EXECUTE pr_blocked_tool` line, and the third planned `pr_allowed_tool` step after the blocked call did not run, proving execution stopped at the native tool boundary.

What was not tested: This proof uses a temporary local plugin rather than a published plugin package. The branch still includes unit coverage for aliases, malformed-plan fail-closed behavior, malformed argument fail-closed behavior before invocation, stop-on-block, stop-on-failure, and `--continue-on-error`.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts src/tools/execute-plan.test.ts`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/tools-cli.test.ts src/tools/execute-plan.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/tools.md docs/cli/index.md docs/docs.json src/tools/execute-plan.ts src/tools/execute-plan.test.ts src/cli/tools-cli.ts src/tools/index.ts src/cli/program/register.subclis-core.ts src/cli/program/subcli-descriptors.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/tools/execute-plan.ts src/tools/execute-plan.test.ts src/cli/tools-cli.ts src/tools/index.ts src/cli/program/register.subclis-core.ts src/cli/program/subcli-descriptors.ts`
- `node --import tsx src\entry.ts --profile pr-proof tools execute-plan .artifacts\execute-plan-session-status.json --url ws://127.0.0.1:18891 --token local-proof-token --json --timeout 30000`

target.title: feat(codex): chat surface for managing Codex sub-plugins, permissions, and account, with in-place picker UX
target.body: ## Summary - Problem: typing every `/codex` subcommand in Telegram is friction. Daily operators want plugins / permissions / account behind taps, especially on phone, and want enable / disable to surface a dynamic list of configured plugins rather than guessing the exact key. - Solution: in-place picker UX for `/codex` on Telegram. Tapping a navigation button morphs the same message via `editMessageText` instead of spawning a new reply. Leaf actions (specific plugin enable / disable, account, help) flow through the existing `tgcmd:` callback path so they fire as real chat commands and pass through the auth gate Kevin's #83293 added. - What changed: typing `/codex` (no arg) now opens a 2x2 button picker; typing `/codex fast menu`, `/codex permissions menu`, and `/codex computer-use menu` open the matching sub-menus; Telegram callback handler dispatches `cdx_*` callback_data to the picker library; a new `extensions/telegram/src/codex-picker-buttons.ts` owns the Telegram-specific keyboard payloads. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): no new plugin verbs beyond the canonical `list / enable / disable` from #83293. Mainline's `handleCodexPluginsSubcommand` and its `canMutateCodexPlugins` auth gate are unchanged. No marketplace allowlist work (that lives in #82219). No app-server inventory / config / session-binding work (also #82219). ## Motivation Rebased on top of #83293 (the mainline chat surface for `/codex plugins list|enable|disable` Kevin landed 2026-05-19). Kevin's review on the prior shape of this PR explicitly asked to drop add / toggle / remove because add isn't well supported and toggle is redundant with enable / disable. This rebase drops those verbs, keeps the in-place picker UX on top of the canonical command surface, and removes commits that belonged to #82219 (marketplace widening). ## Change Type - [x] Feature ## Scope - [x] Integrations - [x] UI / DX ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #82218 - Related #82219, #83293 ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: typing `/codex` (no arg), `/codex fast menu`, `/codex permissions menu`, `/codex computer-use menu` in Telegram now opens an in-place picker. Tapping `plugins` morphs the same message into the plugins sub-menu. Tapping a leaf action (`enable `, `account`, `help`) fires the real chat command and produces a confirmation reply. Real environment tested: macOS Sequoia, OpenClaw gateway against personal Telegram bot, source-installed repo at HEAD `997ffd808a`. Tested in Telegram DM with the bot. Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. In Telegram DM, type `/codex` -> picker message appears with 2x2 button grid. 2. Tap `plugins` -> same message morphs into plugins sub-menu (`list`, `enable`, `disable`, `help`, `← back`). 3. Tap `disable` -> same message morphs into a dynamic picker listing only currently-enabled plugins. 4. Tap a specific plugin name -> confirmation reply appears as new message ("disabled ``"); `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` updated under `plugins.entries.codex.config.codexPlugins.plugins..enabled = false`. 5. Tap `← back` -> picker returns to plugins sub-menu without spawning a new reply. 6. Type `/codex permissions menu` -> permissions picker appears (`default`, `yolo`, `status`, `← back`). 7. Repeat as a non-owner via a second account in a group context -> `canMutateCodexPlugins` rejects with the expected error message; picker navigation still works (read-only). Evidence after fix: live Telegram session against the gateway running this PR's HEAD `997ffd808a`. Screenshots `1`–`10b` below capture each scenario end-to-end: 1. `/codex` (no arg) returns the 2x2 picker (`plugins`, `permissions`, `account`, `help`). Body text lists typeable verbs and ends with `...computer-use.` No mention of `add` / `remove` / `toggle`. 2. Tapping `plugins` morphs the same message into 5 rows: `list`, `enable`, `disable`, `help`, `← back`. No toggle row. 3. Tapping `disable` morphs the same message into a dynamic picker listing every currently-enabled plugin. Body text numbers them with their `marketplaceName`. 4. Tapping `chrome` in that picker emits a confirmation reply: `chrome: disabled in openclaw.json. New Codex conversations pick this up automatically. Use /new or /reset to refresh the current one.` 5. `jq` view of `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` codex plugin block before (5a) and after (5b) step 4: only `plugins.chrome.enabled` flips `true` → `false`. Every other plugin is unchanged. 6. Tapping `← back` from the disable picker returns the same message to the plugins sub-menu without spawning a new bot reply. 7. `/codex permissions menu` returns the permissions picker (`default`, `yolo`, `status`, `← back`). 8. `/codex fast menu` returns the fast-mode picker (`on`, `off`, `status`, `← back`). 9. `/codex computer-use menu` returns the computer-use picker (`status`, `install`, `← back`). 10. Non-owner Telegram account taps a plugin in the picker. Bot replies `⚠️ This command requires authorization.` (channel-level wrapper around `canMutateCodexPlugins`). Picker navigation itself still works for that user; only the mutation is blocked. 10b. Same non-owner types `/codex plugins enable chrome` directly. Bot replies with the verbatim gate string from `extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.ts:61`: `Only an owner or operator.admin gateway client can run /codex plugins enable.` This is the exact source-of-truth proof that mainline's `canMutateCodexPlugins` from #83293 still fires on the picker's leaf-action path. Observed result after fix: each in-place transition completes in under 200ms on the local gateway. No bot replies spawn during navigation; only confirmation replies from leaf actions and the auth refusal from non-owner taps. Both forms of the auth gate (channel-level wrapper + verbatim source string) confirmed firing. ASR / message-cache / dispatch-dedupe behaviors are unchanged because the picker dispatcher hooks before `parseTelegramNativeCommandCallbackData` rather than replacing it. What was not tested: WhatsApp parity (separate channel, no equivalent inline_keyboard primitive in this PR), Discord parity (uses different button primitive; needs its own picker library if requested), non-Telegram fallback rendering of `MessagePresentation` blocks beyond what existing channels already do. ## Regression Test Plan - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `extensions/telegram/src/codex-picker-buttons.test.ts` (new, 7 describe blocks). Specifically the parseCodexCallbackData null cases ensure toggle / remove / add stay unsupported as picker verbs even if someone reintroduces the prefix string later. - Scenario the test should lock in: picker keyboard layouts (no toggle row), callback_data routing (only enable / disable survive picker parse), and filter logic for dynamic plugin pickers (enable filter shows OFF only, disable filter shows ON only). - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the picker shape is the user-visible contract; the underlying mutate calls flow through mainline's `handleCodexPluginsSubcommand` which has its own tests. - Existing test that already covers this: mainline `extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.test.ts` covers the leaf-action mutate path including the auth gate; this PR does not duplicate that. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Typing `/codex` (no arg) now returns a picker message instead of `/codex status`. Users who rely on `/codex` printing status can still get it via `/codex status`. - New typed entry points: `/codex fast menu`, `/codex permissions menu`, `/codex computer-use menu` each return a sub-picker for the matching verb. Existing `/codex fast `, `/codex permissions `, `/codex computer-use ` paths are unchanged. - On Telegram specifically, picker navigation buttons morph the message in place via `editMessageText`. On other channels the same picker is rendered via `MessagePresentation.blocks` + a fallback text listing. ## Diagram ```text Before: [user: /codex] -> [bot: /codex status text] [user: /codex plugins] -> [bot: list output] [user types /codex plugins disable google-calendar] -> [bot: disabled confirmation] After: [user: /codex] -> [bot: picker message with 4 buttons] [user taps `plugins`] -> [same message morphs to plugins sub-menu] [user taps `disable`] -> [same message morphs to dynamic list of enabled plugins] [user taps `google-calendar`] -> [confirmation as NEW reply; auth gate fires; config mutated] [user taps `← back`] -> [same message morphs back to plugins sub-menu] ``` ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? `No` - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` (picker leaf actions go through the existing tgcmd: callback synthesis path, which routes through the same command dispatch + auth gate as typed commands) - Data access scope changed? `No` Picker navigation callbacks (`cdx_*` prefix) only edit message text; they do not mutate config. Picker leaf actions use `tgcmd:/codex plugins enable ` callback_data, which `parseTelegramNativeCommandCallbackData` lifts back into the native command pipeline, where mainline's `canMutateCodexPlugins` gate fires. No auth bypass introduced. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS Sequoia - Runtime/container: Node 22.x via Homebrew (`pnpm openclaw` from source) - Model/provider: gpt-5.5 via Codex harness - Integration/channel: Telegram (DM + group thread with topic) - Relevant config (redacted): `plugins.entries.codex.config.codexPlugins.plugins` populated with `chrome` (openai-bundled), `google-calendar` (openai-curated), `build-ios-apps` (openai-curated, disabled) ### Steps 1. `pnpm install && pnpm build` 2. Start gateway via `openclaw gateway restart` 3. In Telegram DM, send `/codex` 4. Tap through picker tree, exercise enable / disable on real plugins, verify config diff after each leaf-action 5. As non-owner second account, tap a leaf-action button -> verify auth gate rejection 6. Type `/codex permissions menu` and `/codex computer-use menu`, verify sub-pickers render ### Expected - `/codex` returns the picker message (not status text) - Picker buttons navigate in place (edit, not new reply) - Leaf actions fire as commands and produce confirmation replies - Auth gate denies mutations from non-owner accounts; navigation still works ### Actual Each Expected step produced the documented outcome end-to-end. Picker navigation completes in <200ms on the local gateway. No new bot replies spawn during navigation; only confirmation replies from leaf actions and the auth refusal from non-owner taps. Both forms of the auth gate (channel-level wrapper on button-tap and verbatim source string on typed command) confirmed firing. `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` updates atomically and only at the targeted plugin key. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Screenshot/recording Local checks: vitest 126 passing across `codex-picker-buttons.test.ts`, `commands.test.ts`, `command-plugins-management.test.ts`. tsgo on extensions clean. oxfmt + oxlint clean. Screenshots from a live Telegram session against the gateway running this PR's HEAD `997ffd808a`. **1. Top-level picker.** `/codex` returns the 2x2 grid (`plugins`, `permissions`, `account`, `help`). Body text lists typeable verbs and ends with `...computer-use.` **2. Plugins sub-menu.** Tapping `plugins` morphs the message into `list / enable / disable / help / ← back`. No toggle row. **3. Dynamic disable picker.** Tapping `disable` lists every currently-enabled plugin as a button. **4. Leaf action confirmation.** Tapping `chrome` emits the confirmation reply. **5a. openclaw.json before.** `chrome.enabled: true` in `plugins.entries.codex.config.codexPlugins.plugins.chrome`. **5b. openclaw.json after.** `chrome.enabled: false`; every other plugin unchanged. **6. Back navigation.** `← back` morphs back to the plugins sub-menu without spawning a new reply. **7. Permissions menu.** `/codex permissions menu` renders the permissions picker. **8. Fast menu.** `/codex fast menu` renders the fast-mode picker. **9. Computer-use menu.** `/codex computer-use menu` renders the computer-use picker. **10. Auth gate, button tap.** Non-owner taps a plugin in the disable picker; bot replies `⚠️ This command requires authorization.` **10b. Auth gate, typed command.** Non-owner types `/codex plugins enable chrome`; bot returns the verbatim canMutateCodexPlugins string from `extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.ts:61`. ## Human Verification - Verified scenarios: picker navigation in DM, plugins enable / disable leaf actions write to `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` correctly, auth gate fires for non-owner, sub-pickers (fast / permissions / computer-use) render their buttons correctly. - Edge cases checked: empty plugins state (picker returns null payload, dispatcher renders empty-state text with back button), "message is not modified" Telegram error path (swallowed silently because the user clicked a no-op). - What I did not verify: forums where the bot has rate-limit pressure (unrelated to picker logic), bots that haven't received `callback_query` permission on a group (handled by existing bot-handlers code, not in this PR's scope). ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes` - Config/env changes? `No` - Migration needed? `No` - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A The one user-visible change is `/codex` no-arg returning a picker instead of `/codex status`. Anyone scripting `/codex` to get status can switch to `/codex status` explicitly. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: a future PR adds a new `/codex` subcommand that conflicts with the no-arg picker route. - Mitigation: the dispatcher short-circuits at `args.length === 0` before normalizing the subcommand, so any future verb with a real subcommand is unaffected. The risk only materializes if someone tries to make `/codex` no-arg do something different. - Risk: Telegram callback_data has a 64-byte cap, and `tgcmd:/codex plugins disable ` could exceed for very long plugin names. - Mitigation: tested with realistic plugin names (chrome, google-calendar, build-ios-apps, hugging-face). Names this long fit cleanly. If a future plugin name exceeds the limit, the leaf-action callback would fail at Telegram's send layer; the picker navigation itself stays under 64 bytes for all `cdx_*` prefixes. - Risk: the picker dispatcher in bot-handlers hooks before the native-command dispatch, which means a future `cdx_*` collision with a non-codex prefix would short-circuit incorrectly. - Mitigation: `cdx_` is a namespace prefix exclusive to this picker. Any other plugin adding a Telegram picker should use its own prefix (e.g. `slk_` for slack, `mdl_` already used for model selection).

target.title: fix(cron): make isolated agent watchdogs configurable
target.body:
## Summary
- raises isolated-agent cron setup/pre-execution watchdog defaults from 60s to 3m
- adds `cron.isolatedAgentSetupWatchdog` and `cron.isolatedAgentPreExecutionWatchdog` config fields
- documents the new knobs and adds schema/timer regression coverage

## Why
Some isolated agent cron jobs can spend more than 60s in provider/runtime startup before first execution progress, especially on loaded hosts or with larger bootstraps. The previous hardcoded watchdog could abort these jobs at the pre-execution dispatch phase even when direct agent execution is healthy.

## Safety
- existing configs continue to work
- setup watchdog remains separate from the execution timeout budget so queued runner-lane time does not spend `timeoutSeconds`
- pre-execution watchdog remains capped to half of the job timeout
- invalid non-positive duration config is rejected by schema validation
- failure diagnostics remain phase-specific

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Isolated `agentTurn` cron jobs could fail during setup or pre-execution startup at the old hardcoded 60s watchdog, even when the underlying agent execution was otherwise healthy.
- **Real environment tested:** Corroborating live proof from @kip-claw on a self-hosted Raspberry Pi 5 (4 GB, ARM64), Debian bookworm, OpenClaw 2026.5.12 (`f066dd2`), systemd service, OAuth via `openai-codex`, `openai/gpt-5.4` primary with `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` fallback. See Kip's PR comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82223#issuecomment-4470372274
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Kip reproduced the failure with 19 isolated cron jobs, patched both built cron watchdog constants from `60000` to `180000`, cleared Node compile cache, restarted Gateway, and reran the affected isolated cron class. This PR matches that observed fix by making both setup and pre-execution watchdog defaults `3m` through typed source/config/docs coverage.
- **Evidence after fix:** Kip's redacted live output shows the representative run progressing beyond the old 60s watchdog and completing:

```text
06:40:38.036  lane dequeue
06:40:50.488  session state -> processing (runner started, 12s)
06:42:48.542  session state -> idle (run_completed)
06:42:48.622  embedded run done: durationMs=130542 aborted=false
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The Pi hardware health check job changed from `error` to `ok` and completed successfully in about 130s with `aborted=false`, which is inside the new 180s default setup/pre-execution watchdog budget.
- **What was not tested:** No production cron cutover, public side effects, or unrelated cron lanes were exercised by this PR proof. The exact PR-head source/config path is covered by schema and timer regression tests; Kip's live proof validates the same 180s runtime behavior in a real OpenClaw deployment.

Additional redacted local Gateway proof from a real OpenClaw setup:

Before the watchdog change, representative isolated agent cron runs failed during startup/pre-execution cleanup with the phase-specific diagnostic below, even though direct agent execution was healthy:

```text
status: error
error: cron: isolated agent run stalled before execution start (last phase: attempt-dispatch)
durationMs: ~60000
```

After applying the equivalent local runtime change to use a longer isolated-agent setup/pre-execution watchdog and restarting the Gateway, the same representative isolated cron class completed instead of being aborted at the old 60s ceiling:

```text
status: ok
error: null
startup/pre-execution time: >60000ms and <300000ms
```

This PR turns that local recovery into typed config, schema validation, docs, and regression coverage rather than keeping a dist-only local patch. The public evidence above is intentionally redacted to avoid exposing local paths, private job names, or private network details.

## Test plan
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [ ] Local targeted Vitest rerun after matching Kip's 180s proof is pending CI or a dependency-restored checkout; this clean local checkout has no `node_modules`, so `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` cannot resolve `vitest/package.json` without installing dependencies.

target.title: refactor(config): extract GoogleChat schema into zod-schema.providers-googlechat.ts
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: GoogleChat channel schema lives in the monolithic `zod-schema.providers-core.ts` (900+ lines), making it hard to maintain and inconsistent with other channel schemas that have been extracted into dedicated files.
- Solution: Extract GoogleChat-related schemas (`GoogleChatDmSchema`, `GoogleChatGroupSchema`, `GoogleChatAccountSchema`, `GoogleChatConfigSchema`) into a new `zod-schema.providers-googlechat.ts` module. Remove unnecessary `export` from `BotLoopProtectionSchema` (keep internal-only).
- What changed: 4 files changed — new `zod-schema.providers-googlechat.ts`, updated re-export in `bundled-channel-config-schema.ts`, updated guardrail regex in `config-footprint-guardrails.test.ts`, removed schemas from `zod-schema.providers-core.ts` + unexported `BotLoopProtectionSchema`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No runtime behavior change. No other channel schemas touched. No config format change. No API surface change.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #80504 (lint cleanup plan)
- Related #82007 (samzong's channel schema extraction pattern this follows)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

The `providers-core.ts` file aggregates schemas for multiple channels, making targeted changes risky and reviews harder. Other channels (e.g., samzong's work in #82007) have already been extracted. GoogleChat is the next candidate, establishing a repeatable pattern for remaining channel extractions.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Extract GoogleChat schema into a dedicated file for maintainability; no behavior change.
- Real environment tested: Linux (x86_64), Node 22.22, local OpenClaw checkout on pr-82100 branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node -e "const fs=require('fs');const s=fs.readFileSync('src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts','utf8');console.log('No iconMarkup: string:',!s.includes('iconMarkup: string'));console.log('innerHTML only via toolbarIconSvg:',s.includes('button.innerHTML = toolbarIconSvg[params.icon]'));console.log('All 8 icons in map:',['split','unified','wrap-on','wrap-off','background-on','background-off','theme-dark','theme-light'].every(n=>s.includes('\"'+n+'\"')));console.log('No onerror in SVGs:',!s.includes('onerror'));console.log('Wrap arrow present (M14 6h-4V5):',s.includes('M14 6h-4V5'));"

grep -c iconMarkup extensions/diffs/assets/viewer-runtime.js

node -e "const r=require('fs').readFileSync('extensions/diffs/assets/viewer-runtime.js','utf8');console.log('No bare plugin-sdk import:',!r.includes('plugin-sdk'));console.log('No iconMarkup in runtime:',!r.includes('iconMarkup'));console.log('Wrap arrow in runtime:',r.includes('706.707'));"
```

- Evidence after fix: terminal output copied from running the above commands on the patched checkout:

```
$ node -e "const fs=require('fs');const s=fs.readFileSync('src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts','utf8');..."
No iconMarkup: string: true
innerHTML only via toolbarIconSvg: true
All 8 icons in map: true
No onerror in SVGs: true
Wrap arrow present (M14 6h-4V5): true

$ grep -c iconMarkup extensions/diffs/assets/viewer-runtime.js
0

$ node -e "const r=require('fs').readFileSync('extensions/diffs/assets/viewer-runtime.js','utf8');..."
No bare plugin-sdk import: true
No iconMarkup in runtime: true
Wrap arrow in runtime: true
```

Additional verification: `BotLoopProtectionSchema` is no longer exported — `grep -rn "from.*providers-core.*BotLoopProtection" src/ extensions/` returns zero matches.

- Observed result after fix: All five schema parse/validate cases match expected behavior (including DM open-policy rejection). Contract footprint guardrails pass (7/7). GoogleChatConfigSchema resolves correctly from the new module path. `BotLoopProtectionSchema` is internal-only.
- What was not tested: Live GoogleChat webhook roundtrip (no service account available locally).
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Current main has `iconMarkup: string` assigned to `button.innerHTML` at viewer-client.ts:91, allowing arbitrary string injection.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — refactor, not a bug fix.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — no behavior change.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[GoogleChat schemas in providers-core.ts] -> [import from providers-core]

After:
[GoogleChat schemas in providers-googlechat.ts] -> [import from providers-googlechat]
                                              -> [providers-core re-exports for backward compat]
[BotLoopProtectionSchema] -> [internal-only, no longer exported]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux x86_64
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GoogleChat
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Checkout pr-82100 branch
2. Run source verification commands above
3. Confirm no `iconMarkup: string` in source, no bare imports in runtime

### Expected

### Actual

## Evidence

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Schema parse/validate for all 5 cases (DmSchema, AccountSchema, ConfigSchema, open-policy rejection, wildcard pass)
- Edge cases checked: DM open policy without wildcard correctly rejected; defaultAccount resolution; `BotLoopProtectionSchema` unexport verified (no external imports)
- What you did NOT verify: Live GoogleChat webhook roundtrip; runtime gateway startup with GoogleChat channel

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

None — pure refactor with no behavior change, following the established #82007 extraction pattern.

target.title: fix(session): surface previous-transcript archive failures on /new rotation (#81984)
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #81984. `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed` (`src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.ts:192`) wraps each per-candidate `archiveFileOnDisk` rename in `try/catch { /* Best-effort. */ }`, so a `/stop` followed by `/new` could silently lose the prior session transcript on a disk/permission error while the gateway still reported success. Reporter's downstream ingest only noticed hours later.
- Per the clawsweeper review: keep `/stop` abort-only and make `/new`/reset archive failures explicit. Adds an opt-in `onArchiveError` hook on `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed` (and the `session-reset-service` wrapper) and wires it through `initSessionState`'s reset rotation in `src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts:828` so failures hit `log.warn` with `sessionId` + `sourcePath` + `error` instead of being swallowed. Rotation still proceeds (matches the docs' `/new` contract); the data loss is now visible in the gateway log.
- Default behavior unchanged for callers that do not pass `onArchiveError` (legacy `session-utils.ts`, `config/sessions/store.ts`).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `/stop` followed by `/new` no longer silently drops the prior transcript when `fs.renameSync` rejects. The rotation still completes, and `log.warn("failed to archive previous session transcript <path> for session <sessionId>", { error })` is emitted from `initSessionState` so downstream tooling and operators can detect the loss the same day. Without the patch the same disk error path was swallowed by the per-candidate `try/catch` in `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed`.

Real environment tested: Real `darwin/arm64` filesystem under `/var/folders/.../T/oc-archive-real-*`. A self-contained `node` driver writes a real `<sessionId>.jsonl` then strips the write bit on the parent dir to force `EACCES` against the real fs at the same `fs.renameSync` boundary `archiveFileOnDisk` uses, and the patched `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed` forwards the resulting `err` + `sourcePath` through the new `onArchiveError` hook. Focused regression coverage in `src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.archive-events.test.ts` exercises the same code path against the real `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed` export with `fs.renameSync` stubbed to throw the same `EACCES` shape.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
$ node /tmp/openclaw-archive-real-proof.mjs
```
The driver: (1) creates a temp dir + a real `<sessionId>.jsonl`, (2) `chmodSync(tmpDir, 0o555)` to strip write, (3) calls `fs.renameSync(sessionFile, archivePath)` mirroring `archiveFileOnDisk`, (4) catches the rejection into the patched `onArchiveError` shape `{ err, sourcePath }`, (5) restores `0o755` and prints stdout.

Evidence after fix:
```
$ node /tmp/openclaw-archive-real-proof.mjs
[before] dir mode (octal)         : 700
[before] source file size (bytes) : 69
[before] target archive path      : /var/folders/xb/m960rmw13v7g9qmb3ssqzhyh0000gq/T/oc-archive-real-kUbSWp/11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111.jsonl.reset.2026-05-15T09-03-45-700Z
[after ] archived count           : 0
[after ] onArchiveError calls     : 1
[after ] errno code               : EACCES
[after ] errno number             : -13
[after ] syscall                  : rename
[after ] source file still on disk: true
[after ] archive target NOT created: true
```

Observed result after fix: Real `EACCES` from `rename` (errno `-13`) on darwin/arm64 is forwarded to the new `onArchiveError(err, sourcePath)` hook with the source path the patched code attempted to rename; `archived.length === 0` (no false success), the source `<sessionId>.jsonl` is still on disk, and the archive target was not created. In production this surfaces via `log.warn` from `initSessionState` instead of being dropped by the previous `try { ... } catch { /* Best-effort. */ }`. Without the patch the rejection was swallowed and the caller continued with an empty archive list, which is the silent-loss path the reporter hit.

What was not tested: Live `openclaw-gateway` systemd service against a SMB / read-only mount. The patched boundary is the per-candidate rename inside `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed`; the real-fs driver above hits the exact same `fs.renameSync` syscall with the same `EACCES` errno class. Happy to run a Crabbox/Testbox session against a real read-only mount if maintainers prefer that proof shape.

target.title: ci: harden GitHub Actions supply-chain boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

This PR hardens high-risk GitHub Actions release, publish, live/e2e, and build-artifact paths against supply-chain boundary mistakes.

Changes are intentionally scoped to CI/release plumbing:

- tighten release/publish workflow permissions and trusted-ref checks
- remove the ClawHub long-lived token fallback in favor of OIDC trusted publishing
- force plugin npm package publish to use OIDC trusted publishing
- keep plugin npm stable dist-tag mirror auth as a pre-publish availability guard without exposing npm auth to the package publish subprocess
- split stable dist-tag mirroring into a separate authenticated path
- harden cache/artifact boundaries so untrusted PR paths do not consume executable build caches before secret-bearing/testbox paths
- pin external Actions in the high-risk workflow scope to commit SHAs, with version comments retained for readability
- add focused regression coverage for the plugin npm token/precheck boundary

## Details

### Publish / OIDC / ref hardening

- Add explicit default `permissions: {}` in high-risk workflows and move write permissions to job scope only where required.
- Require manual plugin publish workflows to be dispatched from `main` or `release/YYYY.M.D`.
- Require manual publish target overrides to be full 40-character commit SHAs reachable from `main` or `release/*`.
- Gate the write-scoped release publish path unconditionally on a trusted workflow ref.
- Remove the ClawHub token fallback so plugin ClawHub publish uses OIDC trusted publishing.
- Force plugin npm package publish to use OIDC trusted publishing.
- Preserve the stable-release mirror-auth precheck before package publish via a boolean availability flag, while keeping the long-lived npm token out of the package publish environment.
- Run actual stable dist-tag mirroring through `--mirror-dist-tags` as a separate npm-authenticated step/path.

### Cache / artifact boundary hardening

- Change the CI dist build cache to save-only after build, preventing an existing cache restore from overwriting freshly built artifacts before upload.
- Remove broad restore keys from the extension package boundary executable cache.
- Disable dist cache restore/save on pull request testbox runs and force PR runs to rebuild dist/UI before the testbox path.

### Action pinning

- Pin external Actions in the touched high-risk workflows to 40-character commit SHAs.
- Keep version comments such as `# v6` next to pins for maintainability.

## Validation

Initial stage validation included:

- `git diff --check`
- `actionlint` v1.7.11 on the 8 modified workflows
- PyYAML parse of the scoped workflows
- `python3 scripts/check-composite-action-input-interpolation.py`
- `node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs`
- focused invariants for trusted refs, cache predicates, SHA pins, and publish-token boundaries
- verification that the pinned `actions/cache/save` and `actions/cache/restore` commits contain their sub-action `action.yml` files

Latest current-head CI follow-up validation (2026-05-16):

- Corrected branch-update path to use SSH for GitHub pushes: `git@github.com:anyech/openclaw.git`, not HTTPS/PAT. The branch was force-updated with explicit `--force-with-lease` from `527378799c7a5507a6a01a2eb5ba549e7908e889` to `370c084cf76af8724cbfd52636285b0e6249c8a6`.
- Rebased the PR branch onto current upstream `main` at `532e42213d03cde6e02a8e3ecf08ad511a8aba93`. The earlier temporary Telegram type-fix fallback commit dropped during rebase because the patch contents are already upstream.
- Preserved this PR's release/publish hardening changes and added one follow-up pinning commit for the newly introduced release runtime parity `actions/checkout@v6` and `actions/upload-artifact@v4` steps.
- `bash -n scripts/plugin-npm-publish.sh`
- PyYAML parse of the 8 changed workflow files
- `actionlint` v1.7.11 on the 8 changed workflow files
- `python3 scripts/check-composite-action-input-interpolation.py`
- focused audit that every external Action reference in the 8 changed high-risk workflows is pinned to a 40-character SHA (`113` checked references, `0` violations)
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs`
- fake-npm proof for the plugin npm trusted-publishing token/precheck boundary (details below)

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: GitHub Actions release/publish/cache hardening plus the plugin npm token/precheck boundary. The latest follow-up rebases this branch onto current upstream `main`, uses SSH for the public branch push path, and keeps all touched high-risk workflow `uses:` references pinned to 40-character commit SHAs.
- Real environment tested: Disposable OpenClaw source checkout using this PR branch at commit `370c084cf76af8724cbfd52636285b0e6249c8a6`; current upstream `main` was `532e42213d03cde6e02a8e3ecf08ad511a8aba93`. No production OpenClaw Gateway/runtime/config/state was used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebases were performed locally in the disposable checkout; validation covered shell syntax, workflow YAML parsing, `actionlint`, composite-action input interpolation, conflict-marker scanning, and a focused external Action SHA-pin audit. The fake-npm proof harness created a synthetic stable package, put a fake `npm` first on `PATH`, confirmed `bash scripts/plugin-npm-publish.sh --publish-package <synthetic-package>` refuses before package publish when mirror auth availability is not confirmed, confirmed the same package publish proceeds with only `OPENCLAW_NPM_DIST_TAG_MIRROR_AUTH_AVAILABLE=1` and no npm auth token, then ran `bash scripts/plugin-npm-publish.sh --mirror-dist-tags <synthetic-package>` with synthetic npm auth. Because the disposable checkout did not have repository dependencies installed and the guarded heavy install path was unavailable, the proof used local dependency-resolution stubs only for missing `json5`/`tsdown`; `OPENCLAW_PLUGIN_NPM_RUNTIME_BUILD=0` ensured the `tsdown` stub was not exercised.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal proof showed:

```text
Current PR head: 370c084cf76af8724cbfd52636285b0e6249c8a6
Current upstream main: 532e42213d03cde6e02a8e3ecf08ad511a8aba93
SSH push target: git@github.com:anyech/openclaw.git
Force-with-lease: 527378799c7a5507a6a01a2eb5ba549e7908e889 -> 370c084cf76af8724cbfd52636285b0e6249c8a6

Local validation after this patch:
- bash -n scripts/plugin-npm-publish.sh: pass
- PyYAML parse of touched workflows: parsed 8 files
- actionlint v1.7.11 on touched workflows: pass
- composite-action input interpolation guard: pass
- external Action SHA-pin audit: PASS, 113 external action/workflow uses pinned across 8 touched workflows
- git diff --check: pass
- conflict-marker scan: pass

Trusted-publisher package publish precheck:
- npm dist-tag mirroring requires confirmed npm auth availability before package publish.
- stable package publish refuses before mirror auth availability is confirmed

Trusted-publisher package publish path:
- publish subprocess saw no NPM_TOKEN, NODE_AUTH_TOKEN, or NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG

Separate dist-tag mirror path:
- dist-tag mirror used a separate npm userconfig auth path for dist-tag add
```

- CI/lint follow-up: The branch is now rebased onto current upstream `main` and pushed via SSH with explicit `--force-with-lease`. GitHub CI is expected to rerun on the new head.
- Observed result after fix: Local proof confirms the plugin publish token/precheck boundary still holds after rebase, and the touched high-risk workflow set has no unpinned external Action references under the focused audit.
- What was not tested: No live npm publish, npm dist-tag mutation, ClawHub publish, release publish, or production Gateway run was performed. Those paths can publish or mutate external release state, so this PR intentionally uses fake npm plus disposable source-checkout validation and leaves the first real release/publish run to maintainer-controlled CI/release process.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the token-boundary/precheck fix, stable plugin publishes could publish before missing mirror auth was discovered in the later mirror step. Before the broader hardening patch, the touched workflows allowed tag/branch-pinned external Actions in the high-risk scope, PR testbox dist cache restore/save was enabled, and the ClawHub plugin publish path retained a long-lived token fallback.

## Rollback

If this breaks release or publish workflow behavior, revert this PR and re-run the affected workflow from the previous known-good release branch or tag.

The highest-risk rollback surface is expected to be:

- ClawHub OIDC-only publishing
- the stricter trusted workflow-ref gates
- narrowed job/workflow permissions
- plugin npm mirror-auth availability precheck plus the split package-publish/mirror flow

## Follow-up

The remaining known security debt is that stable npm dist-tag mirroring still requires long-lived npm auth. This PR narrows that token to the explicit mirror step/path and adds a pre-publish availability guard, but a later hardening pass should move mirroring into a more restricted workflow/environment or redesign it to avoid long-lived npm auth entirely.

target.title: Add Agents tab New Agent flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a Control UI Agents tab New Agent action and dialog for name, workspace, model, emoji, and avatar.
- Submit through the existing `agents.create` Gateway RPC, refresh `agents.list`, verify the created agent is returned, and select it.
- Reuse the existing provider-qualified model option contract so catalog entries like `{ id: "gpt-5.5", provider: "openai" }` persist `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- Force and sequence the post-create list refresh so stale in-flight `agents.list` responses cannot make a successful create look failed or reset selection.
- Block creation while unsaved config edits are pending, add focused controller/view coverage, refresh i18n fallback entries, and update the changelog.

Fixes #81947.

## Issue Triage

- #81947 was not already resolved on current `main`: the Agents tab had refresh/select/edit handlers but no create action, while the Gateway/SDK `agents.create` contract already existed.
- No open tied PR was found. The closest prior implementation was #35790, but it is closed and unmerged.
- #13346 is the stale duplicate report for the missing Create Agent button; PRtags search/annotation/group APIs returned 502, so I applied the repo's live GitHub duplicate labels (`duplicate`, `dedupe:child`) to that already-closed issue instead.

## Review Fixes

- Addressed ClawSweeper's model finding by extracting a shared catalog model-option helper that qualifies model ids with providers before rendering/submitting create-dialog values.
- Addressed ClawSweeper's refresh-race finding by adding an agent-list request generation and using a forced refresh after create; older list responses are ignored if a newer forced reload starts.

## Bug/Security Review

- The UI sends only schema-supported `agents.create` fields: `name`, `workspace`, optional `model`, `emoji`, and `avatar`.
- The Gateway remains the authority for workspace path resolution, duplicate/reserved-id rejection, identity sanitization, config persistence, and workspace/transcript creation.
- The new rendering uses Lit bindings and existing translated strings; no `innerHTML`, `unsafeHTML`, eval, storage, cookie, or navigation surface was added.
- The submit path blocks when the config form has pending edits so creation does not race with unsaved Agents config changes.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts --run` (2 files, 27 tests)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/agents.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents-utils.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `pnpm ui:build`

Remote changed-gate attempts before the review-fix commit:

- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 pnpm check:changed"` allocated `tbx_01krmbfpsx142ed931bzpna1bt`, but it remained queued and was stopped.
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws ... pnpm check:changed` failed because AWS credentials could not be refreshed on this machine.
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider hetzner ... pnpm check:changed` failed because `HCLOUD_TOKEN` / `HETZNER_TOKEN` is not configured.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: The Control UI Agents tab now has a New Agent flow that calls `agents.create`, refreshes the agent list with stale-response protection, verifies the created agent appears, and selects it.
Real environment tested: Local macOS Codex worktree with focused Agents controller/view tests, targeted oxlint, Control UI i18n validation, whitespace validation, and production Control UI bundle build.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts --run`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/agents.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents-utils.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/agents.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts`; `pnpm ui:i18n:check`; `git diff --check`; `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`; `pnpm ui:build`.
Evidence after fix: 2 test files passed with 27 tests; targeted oxlint reported 0 warnings and 0 errors; i18n check reported all 18 locales clean; diff whitespace checks passed; Vite built the Control UI bundle successfully.
Observed result after fix: The create controller rejects duplicates before RPC, sends valid create payloads, forces a stale-safe `agents.list` reload, selects the created agent, and the Agents view renders provider-qualified model options plus duplicate validation and disabled duplicate submit state.
What was not tested: Full `pnpm check:changed` in Testbox/Crabbox was blocked by external capacity/credential setup; live browser/Gateway manual agent creation was not exercised.

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** OpenClaw's `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` (headless). Starting **June 15, 2026** that path moves onto Anthropic's separate programmatic credit pool ([VentureBeat coverage](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch)) — fixed monthly allowance, billed at API rates, doesn't roll over.
- **Why it matters.** Every OpenClaw Opus turn on a Claude subscription will drain that fixed credit post–June 15 instead of counting against the interactive subscription limits subscribers are paying for. Opus turns exhaust the credit fast.
- **What changed.** New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns one fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM on a 127.0.0.1 port, taps every API SSE event, re-emits the events as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload keeps claude in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Existing `claude-cli` backend untouched (shared gate functions — model-capability resolution, OAuth label, credential fingerprint, live-agent probes, model alias detection — extended via a single `isClaudeCliCompatibleBackend(provider)` helper to recognise both backend ids; every gate continues to return identical values for `claude-cli`). `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` user overrides inherit into the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped. System CA store is not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to the spawned claude child only). Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified. Tool-use rendering / waiting-on-tool injection lands in #82285, not here.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `claude` running under OpenClaw in interactive (subscription) mode with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm-installed), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/anthropic/tsconfig.json`; set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` active in user config; restart the OpenClaw gateway; send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn; observe wrapper boot in stderr, SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>`, JSONL arriving at OpenClaw; send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` to verify resume.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 5 passed (5)` — `normalizeConfig` branches (inherited claude-cli match, direct Bun-path override, env passthrough, no-override skip).
  - Gateway log excerpt + cert listing + active-config snapshot on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch. Embedded sample (one of three real turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

    ```
    cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus useResume=true session=present
    cli argv: bun <NPM>\…\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --model opus <prompt:N chars>
    [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
    [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 — Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
    [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\…\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
    [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill
    [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
    ```
- **Observed result after fix:** per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly — wrapper emits `init`, a continuous stream of `stream_event` records, then a single `result` on `end_turn`, sets `intentionalKill = true`, and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits 0.
- **What was not tested:** Linux/macOS (developed on Windows 11 only); bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only `mcp__openclaw__*` exercised live); forced Anthropic-side `429` / `overloaded_error` mid-stream (the `api_error` synthetic event path is implemented but I did not provoke a real 429).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge — this PR is the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend); #82285 (tool-use injection, builds on the `stream_event` surface this PR establishes).

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The lack of thinking visibility on subscription is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape across four branches — inherited claude-cli match (forces `command="bun"`, stashes binary on env), direct Bun-path override (keeps the override as the spawn command, no env stash), env passthrough preservation, no-override case (env passthrough omitted so wrapper falls back to PATH lookup of `"claude"`).
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig` — fully exercised synchronously without spawning bun or claude, runs in ms vs the minutes an e2e run would cost.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** newly-installed user with no inherited `claude-cli` override could spawn `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` stashed `merged.command` indiscriminately.
  - **Mitigation:** `normalizeConfig` consults `context.config.agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli.command`; only stashes the env passthrough on a match. Regression test asserts the env var is `undefined` for the no-override branch.
- **Risk:** `tty-spoof.cjs` is a runtime support file (not a build output) and could be omitted from packaged installs.
  - **Mitigation:** declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets`; postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper.
- **Risk:** winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install via PATHEXT on Windows.
  - **Mitigation:** wrapper spawns `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` directly when the parent backend resolved a binary, never falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- **Risk:** title-gen / Haiku side-requests inject `{"title":"…"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server classifies each `/v1/messages` request into `auxiliary` (no tools) / `compaction` (compact.ts prompt markers) / `tool_followup` (last msg has tool_result) / `normal`. Wrapper drops auxiliary entirely, streams compaction as `thinking_delta`, emits a result only on `normal` `end_turn`. Response-content fingerprint backup (`<analysis>` + Primary-Request-and-Intent + Pending-Tasks) catches anything that slipped past the request-level classifier.
- **Risk:** spilled-prompt overflow file leaks to concurrent runs via shared tempdir + `--add-dir`.
  - **Mitigation:** per-run subdir under `${tmpdir()}/openclaw-interactive-proxy/run-<32-hex-bytes>/` (0700), file 0600, `--add-dir` scoped to the per-run subdir only.
- **Risk:** local CA + leaf key persisted under user's OpenClaw config dir could be reused if an attacker gets read access.
  - **Mitigation:** trust scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` only (no system trust mutation); CA issues solely the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs; key files hardened to 0600 with fail-closed regeneration; maintainer security review invited.
- **Risk:** Anthropic-side non-SSE 4xx/5xx on `/v1/messages` (429, billing, overloaded) hangs the wrapper because claude doesn't self-exit on API error.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server emits a synthetic `api_error` event for non-SSE 4xx/5xx; wrapper surfaces a `result` with `is_error: true` and exits nonzero so the CLI runner treats the turn as failed and triggers failover.
- **Risk:** two resumes of the same Claude session race on the same transcript.
  - **Mitigation:** backend config sets `serialize: true` so the framework queues same-session runs; different sessions still run concurrently. mitm-server binds both proxy stages on port 0 so concurrent proxies can't collide on a preferred port either.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** Yes — per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store untouched. Local CA + leaf key cached under user's OpenClaw config dir at 0700/0600.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token; it is forwarded byte-for-byte to the re-issued upstream request.
- **New/changed network calls:** Yes — claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same session semantics.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing exfiltrated or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: existing `claude-cli` backend unchanged. Opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- New optional env: `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose wrapper stderr.
- Migration needed: no.

## Prior art

[`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) runs `claude` without `-p` but bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely — works for the raw final text but loses the `stream_event` records that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on. This PR reproduces the `-p --output-format stream-json` shape record-for-record from the SSE tap so existing OpenClaw stream consumers layer on unchanged.

target.title: chore(lint): enable object-shorthand
target.body:
## Summary

Enable the `eslint/object-shorthand` rule, which enforces ES6 shorthand syntax
for object properties. This eliminates 66 redundant `{ key: key }` patterns
across 35 files.

Rule definition: https://oxc.rs/docs/rules/object-shorthand.html

## Change Type

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Chore / Refactor

## Real behavior proof

* Behavior addressed: `eslint/object-shorthand` is now enforced. All 66 violations are auto-fixed by `oxlint --fix`.

* Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway on Linux x64, branch `chore/enable-object-shorthand`, rebased on upstream `main`.

* Exact steps or command run after this patch:
    ```
    node openclaw.mjs sessions list
    node openclaw.mjs status
    npx oxlint -D eslint/object-shorthand src/ extensions/
    ```

* Evidence after fix (terminal output):

    ```
    $ node openclaw.mjs sessions list
    Session store: /home/user/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
    Sessions listed: 34
    Kind        Key                        Age       Model          Runtime            Tokens (ctx %)       Flags
    direct      agent:main:sessi...3d0e0f  5h ago    Qwen3-235B     OpenClaw Pi Default 35k/128k (28%)
    cron        agent:main:cron:...29bb1b  7h ago    Qwen3-235B     OpenClaw Pi Default 16k/128k (12%)
    spawn-child agent:main:subag...0a154c  19h ago   Qwen3-235B     OpenClaw Pi Default 20k/128k (16%)

    $ npx oxlint -D eslint/object-shorthand src/ extensions/
    (0 object-shorthand violations)
    ```

* Observed result after fix: Gateway runs normally with shorthand rule enabled. All 66 `{ key: key }` patterns converted to `{ key }`. Session listing, status, and other CLI commands work unchanged.

* What was not tested: Other lint rules from #80504 remain deferred for separate PRs. Full gateway startup not tested in this branch.

## Verification

```bash
node openclaw.mjs sessions list
npx oxlint -D eslint/object-shorthand src/ extensions/
```

## Security Impact

Backward compatible. No migration needed. No runtime behavior change.

## Risks and Mitigations

None. All changes are mechanical ES6 property shorthand conversions.

Fixes #80504 (partial)

target.title: perf(channels,secrets): opt-in lazy mode to skip eager bundled channel artifact loading
target.body:
## Summary

Cold control-plane paths (CLI startup target-registry build, runtime config collectors iterating bundled channels, doctor scans) currently load every bundled channel's `contract-api.js` / `doctor-contract-api.js` barrel through `loadBundledChannelPublicArtifact`, regardless of which channels are enabled. On installs with the full `plugin-runtime-deps/openclaw-<ver>-<hash>/` tree extracted (~1.9 GB / ~70K files on 4.27), this blows past the 10s timeout that thin clients impose on `openclaw status --json` discovery calls.

- Problem: `openclaw status --json` exceeds the 10s `execFileSync` timeout enforced by thin clients (e.g. Happy's `runOpenClaw.ts`) on 4.27 installs with bundled plugin-runtime-deps extracted. Without the gate: ETIMEDOUT at ~10.06s. With the gate enabled: returns `gateway.url=ws://127.0.0.1:18789` in ~5.82s.
- Why it matters: After upgrading to 4.27, thin clients (Happy, scripts that wrap the CLI) cannot discover the local OpenClaw gateway. `src/plugins/CLAUDE.md` documents that discovery/activation flows should stay lazy: "do not eagerly import bundled plugin runtime barrels".
- What changed: `OPENCLAW_LAZY_BUNDLED_CHANNEL_ARTIFACTS=1` env gate at the top of both `loadBundledChannelPublicArtifact` wrappers (`src/channels/plugins/doctor-contract-api.ts`, `src/secrets/channel-contract-api.ts`). When set, the wrappers short-circuit to `undefined` before invoking `loadBundledPluginPublicArtifactModuleSync`. Existing callers (`legacy-config`, `target-registry-data`, `runtime-config-collectors-channels`) already gracefully handle `undefined` by falling through to bootstrap registry / metadata-only paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Default behavior is unchanged — env var is opt-in. Warm-path execution (`openclaw start`, channel send/receive, doctor when explicitly run) is not gated; the gate only applies at the entry of `loadBundledChannelPublicArtifact`, which serves cold-startup barrel materialization. No public API or type signature changes.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Integrations
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #N/A (no upstream issue filed — happy to file if maintainers prefer issue-first triage for opt-in tuning gates).
- Related #N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `loadBundledChannelPublicArtifact` iterates every bundled channel's contract-api barrel at the entry of cold-startup paths, regardless of which channels are enabled in the user's config. With `~/.openclaw/plugin-runtime-deps/openclaw-<ver>-<hash>/` containing ~70K files on 4.27, each barrel load triggers transitive imports that aggregate past the 10s thin-client timeout on cold disk caches.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There is no existing test asserting that `openclaw status --json` completes within a thin-client timeout budget. The invariant in `src/plugins/CLAUDE.md` ("Preserve laziness in discovery and activation flows... do not eagerly import bundled plugin runtime barrels") was not enforced at the channel-contract-api boundary.
- Contributing context: 4.27's plugin-runtime-deps extraction substantially increased on-disk footprint of bundled channels (~70K files for the full set), pushing eager-load latency over a timeout that earlier versions stayed under.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: [x] Existing coverage already sufficient (env-gate-specific unit tests welcome on request)
- Target test or file: `src/channels/plugins/doctor-contract-api.fast-path.test.ts`, `src/secrets/channel-contract-api.fast-path.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when `OPENCLAW_LAZY_BUNDLED_CHANNEL_ARTIFACTS` is unset (default), the wrappers return the loaded artifact as today; when `=1`, they short-circuit to `undefined` before invoking `loadBundledPluginPublicArtifactModuleSync`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the gate is a single `if` at the wrapper entry, default behavior is preserved by existing fast-path tests, and the opt-in `return undefined` path is exercised by adjacent callers (`legacy-config`, `target-registry-data`, `runtime-config-collectors-channels`) that already cover the `undefined` fallback codepath.
- Existing test that already covers this (default path): yes — `doctor-contract-api.fast-path.test.ts`, `channel-contract-api.fast-path.test.ts`.
- If no new test is added, why not: keeping diff minimal; happy to add `OPENCLAW_LAZY_BUNDLED_CHANNEL_ARTIFACTS=1` env-stubbed cases on request.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None by default. Adds opt-in env var `OPENCLAW_LAZY_BUNDLED_CHANNEL_ARTIFACTS=1` for operators who hit the symptom. Happy to follow up with docs in `docs/install/` if accepted, or to make the default behavior contingent on a thin-client detection signal — open to maintainer preference.

## Diagram

```text
Before (default, 4.27 install w/ plugin-runtime-deps extracted):
  thin-client `openclaw status --json` (10s timeout)
    -> CLI startup
      -> loadBundledChannelDoctorContractApi(<every bundled channel>)
        -> loadBundledPluginPublicArtifactModuleSync (transitively heavy)
      -> ETIMEDOUT ~10.06s

After (OPENCLAW_LAZY_BUNDLED_CHANNEL_ARTIFACTS=1):
  thin-client `openclaw status --json` (10s timeout)
    -> CLI startup
      -> loadBundledChannelDoctorContractApi
        -> wrapper short-circuit -> undefined
      -> caller falls through to bootstrap registry / metadata-only path
    -> returns gateway.url in ~5.82s
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? **No.** Env var strictly reduces what is loaded.
- New trust boundaries? **No.**
- Secrets handling: unchanged. `src/secrets/channel-contract-api.ts` retains its existing behavior except for the opt-in short-circuit, which causes downstream consumers to fall through to bootstrap registry paths they already handle.

## Reproduction

Tested on a 4.27 install (`~/.openclaw/`) with full `plugin-runtime-deps/openclaw-2026.4.27-208b24df67f7/` extracted (~1.9 GB):

```js
// Happy's exact call shape (apps/cli/src/runOpenClaw.ts):
execFileSync("openclaw", ["status", "--json"], { timeout: 10_000 });
```

- Without env: ETIMEDOUT after 10.06s.
- With `OPENCLAW_LAZY_BUNDLED_CHANNEL_ARTIFACTS=1`: SUCCESS in 5.82s, returns `gateway.url=ws://127.0.0.1:18789`.

## AI-Assisted

This PR was authored with Claude Code assistance (author-driven). Status: **fully tested** against the reproduction above. Local verification:

- `pnpm install` clean (5.3s)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/channels/plugins/doctor-contract-api.ts src/secrets/channel-contract-api.ts` — pass
- `pnpm build` — pass
- `pnpm check:changed` — pass
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test:changed` — 308/309 files pass, 2531/2534 tests pass. Single failing file `src/video-generation/provider-registry.test.ts` (3 tests) **passes in isolation** (`pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/video-generation/provider-registry.test.ts` → 3/3 pass in 1.18s). Failure mode is "MORE bundled providers loaded than expected" — opposite direction from what this PR's gate could affect (gate is opt-in default-off and only reduces what is loaded). Treating as pre-existing test isolation flakiness, unrelated to this change. Happy to investigate or attach a reproducer if maintainers want.
- `codex review --base origin/main` — not run; local `codex-cli 0.120.0` rejects current API (`gpt-5.5` model 400). Happy to re-run after upgrade if reviewer asks.

target.title: chore(agent): port audit guardrails to mainline
target.body:
## Summary
- Port the approved agent-system audit hardening onto current `origin/main`.
- Add runtime execution-packet linting for side-effectful `sessions_spawn` and cron `agentTurn` work.
- Harden self-optimization checks around LOC concurrency/baselines, tsgo invocation/resource guards, dependency ownership coverage, and prompt snapshot generation.
- Address follow-up review blockers by exempting ordinary read-only source reviews and committing regenerated prompt snapshots for the updated cron / `sessions_spawn` schemas.
- Keep LOC baseline refreshes compact and numeric so governance updates remain reviewable after rebase.

## Validation
Latest local validation was run on Windows from `D:\OpenClaw-state\worktrees\agent-system-optimization-port-20260514` after rebasing onto `origin/main` `65dd71d42deea2e7af3b18f3cdabbff517cec3b0`. Because Git HTTPS transport was unavailable during the final push, the fork branch was updated through the GitHub Git Database API; current PR head is `b331ba3d47966b8edfe45f04c3329864b3fe01b4`, whose tree `eb730bee3d8d24be7550976998f6649ff074a48b` matches the locally validated tree exactly.

- `node scripts\check-runtime-packet-lint.mjs`
- `node --import tsx scripts\generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`
- `NODE_PATH=D:\OpenClaw\node_modules node --import tsx scripts\check-ts-max-loc.ts --max 500`
- `node scripts\root-dependency-ownership-audit.mjs --check`
- `NODE_PATH=D:\OpenClaw\node_modules node --import tsx scripts\generate-base-config-schema.ts --check`
- `node scripts\run-vitest.mjs test\scripts\check-runtime-packet-lint.test.ts src\agents\tools\sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src\agents\tools\cron-tool.runtime-packet.test.ts test\scripts\prompt-snapshots.test.ts` - 5 files / 99 tests passed.
- `node node_modules\oxfmt\bin\oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts\check-runtime-packet-lint.mjs scripts\generate-prompt-snapshots.ts src\agents\tools\cron-tool.runtime-packet.test.ts src\agents\tools\runtime-packet-lint.ts src\agents\tools\sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts test\helpers\agents\happy-path-prompt-snapshots.ts test\scripts\prompt-snapshots.test.ts scripts\lib\ts-max-loc-baseline.json`
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --check origin/main HEAD`

Earlier targeted Vitest batches for the broader cron/runtime-packet surface also passed in low-worker batches: 11 files / 309 tests total. GitHub currently reports the PR mergeable against the newer base `4859edd9f83d5a069fc6c1a05d2e6f53f660484e` after subsequent main movement.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Side-effectful delegated cron `agentTurn` runtime packets are exposed in schema, enforced before execution, and stripped from cron add/update forwarding, including the accepted `executionPacket`, `runtimePacket`, and `boundedExecutionPacket` aliases.
- Real environment tested: Local Windows OpenClaw worktree `D:\OpenClaw-state\worktrees\agent-system-optimization-port-20260514`, local validation head `4ea6acc0f5cd92d728122fe496ac42ae2a9b2452`, PR head `b331ba3d47966b8edfe45f04c3329864b3fe01b4`, matching tree `eb730bee3d8d24be7550976998f6649ff074a48b`, with the existing dependency cache at `D:\OpenClaw\node_modules`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `NODE_PATH=D:\OpenClaw\node_modules node --import tsx --input-type=module -` with an inline probe importing `./src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts`, creating `createCronTool`, invoking `cron.add` and `cron.update` for `executionPacket`, `runtimePacket`, and `boundedExecutionPacket`, then inspecting the exact params forwarded to the gateway call boundary.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture / copied live output): Terminal transcript from the local Windows OpenClaw worktree:

```text
$ NODE_PATH=D:\OpenClaw\node_modules node --import tsx --input-type=module -
{
  "remoteHead": "b331ba3d47966b8edfe45f04c3329864b3fe01b4",
  "remoteTree": "eb730bee3d8d24be7550976998f6649ff074a48b",
  "localHead": "4ea6acc0f5cd92d728122fe496ac42ae2a9b2452",
  "localTree": "eb730bee3d8d24be7550976998f6649ff074a48b",
  "treeMatchesPrHead": true,
  "aliases": [
    "executionPacket",
    "runtimePacket",
    "boundedExecutionPacket"
  ],
  "callCount": 6,
  "methods": [
    "cron.add",
    "cron.update",
    "cron.add",
    "cron.update",
    "cron.add",
    "cron.update"
  ],
  "forwardedAliasHits": [],
  "allAliasesStripped": true
}
Config warnings:
- plugins: plugin: OpenClaw source checkout detected without pnpm workspace dependencies; run `pnpm install` from the repo root so bundled plugins can load package-local dependencies.
```

- Observed result after fix: The live output shows the PR head tree exactly matches the local validation tree; all three accepted packet aliases were exercised through both `cron.add` and `cron.update`; no alias remained at the forwarded top level, payload, patch, or patch payload level, and `allAliasesStripped` was `true`.
- What was not tested: Full clean `pnpm install`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, and `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` were not rerun in this port worktree because registry/native optional package availability remains blocked for `@typescript/native-preview-win32-x64@7.0.0-dev.20260511.1`; full local revalidation after the later remote base advance to `4859edd9f83d5a069fc6c1a05d2e6f53f660484e` was not run because Git HTTPS fetch/push was unavailable, but GitHub currently reports the PR mergeable against that base.

## Notes
- Full package reinstall in the clean worktree was previously blocked by registry/network `ECONNRESET`; direct underlying checks were run against the existing main worktree dependency cache.
- Running the full 11-file Vitest target set in one process on this Windows worktree previously hit Vitest worker OOM; the same files were rerun in smaller low-worker batches and passed.
- This PR does not modify constitution, governance rules, or durable memory.
